rulings.Case Title: CESAR ASCARRUNZ VS. HOWARD HSU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Millie Luong'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Of Third Amended Complaint (Complaint Filed 2-6-09), For Failure To Bring To Trial In Five Years; Date: June 16, 2014 Time: 9:30 A.M. Location: Dept. 302 (Res. 051214-08) C.C.P. Sec. 583.310; 583.360 Trial Date Never Set
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 30, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT MILLIE LUONG'S Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Of Third Amended Complaint (Complaint Filed 2-6-09), For Failure To Bring To Trial In Five Years; C.C.P. §§ 583.310, 583.360 Trial Date Never Set. The motion is granted and the court dismisses defendant Millie Luong. Plaintiff failed to bring this action to trial within 5 years after commencing the action against Ms. Luong. Dismissal is mandatory. See CCP 583.310; 583.360. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate that there is a basis to toll the 5-year period pursuant to CCP 583.340. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TSHAITU DAVIS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Sumary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 30, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is denied, and its Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The Motion is granted as to Counts One and Two for negligence and willful failure to warn. The Motion is denied as to Count Three for dangerous condition of public property. The sidewalk defect is not trivial as a matter of law and there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether the defect constituted a "dangerous condition." (See Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts in Opp. # 4, 10.) Additionally, Plaintiff produced evidence-which Defendant disputes-that the defect has existed for a considerable amount of time. (See Moritz Decl. 16.) "[I]t is a question of fact for the jury to determine whether the condition complained of has existed for a sufficient time to give the public agency constructive notice." (Erfurt v. State of Cal. (1983) 141 Cal.App.3d 837, 844-45. See also Straughter v. State of California (1976) 89 Cal.App.3d 102, 111 ("The question of whether the dangerous condition should have been discovered by reasonable inspection and whether there is adequate time for preventive measures is properly left to the jury.").) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Depositions Of Deft Sun Life Assurance Company Of Canada'S Person(S) Most Knowledgeable, Lisa Bonajuto, And For Production Of Documents At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 30, 2014, Line 6, PLAINTIFF KENNETH RADER'S Motion To Compel Depositions Of Defendant Sun Life Assurance Company Of Canada's Person(s) Most Knowledgeable, Lisa Bonajuto, And For Production Of Documents At Deposition. The motion is granted in part. The deposition of Ms. Bonaiuto shall occur by July 7, 2014. The deposition shall take place in Massachusetts or by video if plaintiff prefers. Defendant shall turnover all documents in response to request 21. The deposition of the PMK is denied. The request is untimely, defendant would be prejudiced with another deposition given the close trial date and trial preparation, and much of the information regarding defendant's bonus structure can be determined at Ms. Bonaiuto's deposition. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA MORENO et al VS. DEIRDRE FAHERTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf'S To Respond To Deft'S Interrogatories And Req.'S For Production And Req. For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, June 30, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANT DEIRDRE FAHERTY's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's To Respond To Defendant'S Interrogatories And Request For Production And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is granted, and sanctions are awarded to the moving party in the amount of $1,215, which omits the estimated fees for replying to an opposition (no opposition was filed). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to TSchoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN QUINN VS. AECHELON TECHNOLOGY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 30, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT AECHELON TECHNOLOGY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. The Amended Complaint sufficiently alleges consideration and the terms of the oral contract. Plaintiff alleges that the parties entered into the compensation agreement on January 23, 2006, defendant agreed to compensate plaintiff $20,000 annually for his service as a director of defendant, and plaintiff was not paid for the 2011-2012 term. The allegations regarding 2013 relate to how defendant restructured the compensation into two components. Plaintiff does not clearly allege a new agreement. The court reads the Amended Complaint liberally under CCP 452. The court did not consider plaintiff's opposing declaration in rendering its ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Docum,Ents To Deft Justice Investors, Set One, To Compel Production Of Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, June 30, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFF EVON CORPORATION's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Docum,Ents To Deft Justice Investors, Set One, To Compel Production Of Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions Continued on the court's own motion to July 7, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE KIHAGI VS. KELLY KIMBALL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Anne Kihagi Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 30, 2014, Line 13, PLAINTIFF ANNE KIHAGI'S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication. Off calendar as moot. On June 26, 2014, the Court granted Defendant Kelly Kimball's Motion for Summary Judgment and entered judgment in favor of Defendant Kelly Kimball and against Plaintiff Anne Kihagi. =(302/EHG)
